steps.title: Find the center of symmetry
steps.content:
Absolute value equations $|f(x)| = k$ have solutions symmetric around where $f(x) = 0$. Solving $5x - 5 = 0$:
$$5x = 5$$
$$x = 1$$
The two solutions are equidistant from $x = 1$.

steps.title: Calculate the sum using symmetry
steps.content: When two values are equidistant from a center point, their average equals that center. Since the center is $x = 1$, the average of the two solutions is 1, so their sum is $2 \times 1 = 2$.
